name: Lagoons Route
name_original: Deltebre, El Poblenou del Delta – ruta circular
description: An easy 15.7-mile touring cycling route through the Ebro Delta, offering flat terrain, diverse scenery, and birdwatching opportunities.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Amposta is characterized by the remarkably flat landscapes of the Ebro Delta Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The region features vast expanses of rice fields, numerous lagoons like Encanyissada and Tancada, and the Ebro River itself. These natural features create an ideal environment for cycling, offering diverse scenery and well-established routes. Best touring cycling routes around Amposta The most popular touring cycling route is Casa Fusta, San Carlos de la Rápita, Ullals de Baltasar – El Poblenou del Delta – circular route, a 19.1 miles (30.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 6 minutes to complete. This route explores the unique Ullals de Baltasar natural springs and the coastal areas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Deltebre, El Poblenou del Delta – circular route, an easy 15.7 miles (25.3 km) path. This route offers views of the Ebro Delta's flat terrain and rice fields, often completed in about 1 hour 27 minutes. Local touring cyclists also love the Amposta to the Encanyissada – cycle touring in Catalonia, a 35.0 miles (56.3 km) trail leading through the Ebro Delta and past the Encanyissada lagoon, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Touring cycling around Amposta is defined by the flat terrain of the Ebro Delta, vast rice fields, and numerous lagoons. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family-friendly rides to more moderate excursions. The routes in Amposta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 500 reviews. More than 3000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Amposta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Amposta?
A: There are over 100 touring cycling routes available around Amposta, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and some more challenging routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Amposta?
A: The Ebro Delta region, including Amposta, is ideal for cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are mild and the natural landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, while winter offers cooler, often sunny days, though some services might have reduced hours.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Amposta?
A: The touring cycling routes around Amposta are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 500 reviews. Cyclists often praise the remarkably flat terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Ebro Delta, and the abundance of birdlife.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Amposta?
A: Yes, Amposta and the Ebro Delta are perfect for easy rides. Nearly half of the routes are classified as easy, making them ideal for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974717795/deltebre-el-poblenou-del-delta-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deltebre, El Poblenou del Delta – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 15.7 miles (25.3 km) and offers gentle terrain through rice fields.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes around Amposta?
A: While the Ebro Delta is predominantly flat, there are some routes that offer a more moderate challenge, often due to their length or exposure to wind. About 16 of the routes are classified as difficult, providing longer distances or slightly more varied terrain, sometimes with views towards the Montsià mountains in the distance.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the Amposta area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Amposta are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974859424/casa-fusta-san-carlos-de-la-rapita-ullals-de-baltasar-el-poblenou-del-delta-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Fusta, San Carlos de la Rápita, Ullals de Baltasar – El Poblenou del Delta – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 19.1-mile (30.8 km) loop exploring the unique Ullals de Baltasar natural springs.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around Amposta?
A: Cycling around Amposta offers unique landscapes dominated by the Ebro Delta Natural Park. You'll traverse vast rice fields, cycle alongside numerous lagoons like Encanyissada and Tancada, and follow paths along the Ebro River. The terrain is remarkably flat, providing expansive views of wetlands and diverse ecosystems.

Q: Are there any notable natural attractions or viewpoints along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The Ebro Delta is rich in natural attractions. You can visit the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4583989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullals de Baltasar&lt;/a&gt; natural springs, or stop at birdwatching hides like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/979695" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tancada Birdwatching Hide&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4121762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flamingo Viewing Area&lt;/a&gt; to observe the abundant birdlife, including flamingos. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/979684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Carreter&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic views.

Q: What cultural or historical sights can I explore near the cycling routes in Amposta?
A: Amposta itself features notable modernist architecture and the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3978044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont Penjant&lt;/a&gt; (Suspension Bridge) over the Ebro River. Further afield, you can explore sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde del Baix Ebre&lt;/a&gt;, a greenway built on a former railway line, or visit the Ebro Delta Museum to learn about the local history and ethnography.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching while cycling in the Ebro Delta?
A: The Ebro Delta is one of Europe's most important wetlands and a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, making it a prime spot for birdwatching. As you cycle, you'll pass numerous lagoons and reed beds, which are crucial habitats for a diverse array of birdlife, including large flocks of flamingos. Many routes pass directly by designated viewing areas.

Q: Is it possible to reach the cycling routes around Amposta using public transport?
A: Amposta is accessible by public transport, primarily by bus and train, which can get you to the town center. From there, many cycling routes are easily reachable, especially those that start directly in Amposta or its immediate surroundings. Check local transport schedules for specific connections to trailheads.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a cycling tour in Amposta?
A: Amposta offers various public parking options within the town, often near the river or main attractions. When starting a tour, look for designated parking areas in Amposta or at popular starting points within the Ebro Delta Natural Park, such as near Casa de Fusta or El Poblenou del Delta, which often have visitor parking.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes, especially those passing through villages or popular visitor areas within the Ebro Delta, offer opportunities to stop for refreshments. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and small shops in towns like Deltebre, El Poblenou del Delta, and Sant Carles de la Ràpita, as well as at specific points of interest like Casa de Fusta.

Q: Are touring cycling routes around Amposta suitable for dogs?
A: While dogs are generally welcome in outdoor areas, it's important to keep them on a leash, especially within the Ebro Delta Natural Park, to protect the sensitive wildlife and agricultural areas. Some routes might be more suitable than others depending on the dog's fitness and the presence of shade or water sources. Always carry water for your pet.

Q: Which route explores the Encanyissada lagoon?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1285572165/amposta-to-the-encanyissada-cycle-touring-in-catalonia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amposta to the Encanyissada – cycle touring in Catalonia&lt;/a&gt; is a 35.0 miles (56.3 km) trail that specifically leads through the Ebro Delta and past the Encanyissada lagoon, offering excellent views of this significant wetland.
tips: Here we find a magnificent belvedere to observe the flamingos. The Ebro is the only place where this species nests all year round. An open-air fresco!
There is an excellent viewpoint to see the lagoon and the birds that live there. Around there are a couple of restaurants and bars if necessary.

The viewpoint is located at the junction point of the two parts of l'Encanyissada. It is a great place to see birds - the Ebro Delta has become an international ornithological landmark in the southern Mediterranean.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is specifically noted as an "easy bike ride." Its flat terrain and minimal elevation gain make it ideal for all fitness levels, including beginners and families looking for a relaxed touring experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features largely flat terrain with no significant slopes. You'll cycle on a mix of paved surfaces and earth or dirt tracks, providing a smooth touring experience through the Ebro Delta Natural Park.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this route?
faqs.answer: This 25.3 km route can typically be completed in about 1 hour and 27 minutes. However, it's designed to be enjoyed at a leisurely pace, allowing plenty of time to stop and appreciate the scenery and wildlife.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact starting point are not detailed, the Ebro Delta region is cyclist-friendly. You can often find parking in towns like El Poblenou del Delta, which is a good base for accessing circular routes in the area. Look for designated parking near local amenities.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and wildlife can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll be immersed in the diverse ecosystems of the Ebro Delta, featuring rivers, bays, beaches, lagoons, and vast rice fields. It's a crucial wetland area, so expect excellent opportunities for birdwatching. The changing colors of the rice fields add to the scenic appeal throughout the year.

faqs.question: Are there any specific highlights or viewpoints on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, along the way, you can visit the La Tancada Birdwatching Hide for excellent birdwatching opportunities. You might also pass by the La Casa de Fusta Restaurant & Visitor Center and the Mirador del Través, offering great views of the Delta landscape.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The generally favorable weather conditions in the Ebro Delta mean this route can be enjoyed year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, while the changing colors of the rice fields provide unique beauty in different seasons.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Ebro Delta Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or to pay an entrance fee to cycle this route within the Parc Natural del Delta de l'Ebre. Access to the park's cycling paths is generally free and open to the public.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the Ebro Delta Natural Park is a protected area, dogs are generally permitted on trails if kept on a leash. Always ensure your dog is under control and that you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route integrates with other paths in the Ebro Delta. It covers significant sections of the Ruta de les llacunes and the Camí de Sant Jaume, and also briefly touches upon parts of the Amposta - Ullals - Casa de Fusta and la Ràpita - Casa de Fusta trails.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or rent bikes near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the region is well-equipped for cyclists. You'll find restaurants like La Casa de Fusta Restaurant & Visitor Center along the route. Many accommodations in El Poblenou del Delta, such as Hotel L'Algadir del Delta, cater to cyclists, offering bike rentals (including electric options), mechanical assistance, and secure parking.

faqs.question: What should I consider if cycling this route during warmer months?
faqs.answer: During the height of summer, caution is advised due to the potential lack of shade along many sections of the route. It's recommended to start early in the morning or late in the afternoon, carry plenty of water, and wear sun protection.

waypoints.name: La Casa de Fusta Restaurant & Visitor Center
